name: John Heard
abstract: John William Heard (July 3, 1938 – December 10, 2021) was an American bass player and artist. His recording credits include albums with Pharoah Sanders, George Duke, Oscar Peterson, Count Basie, Zoot Sims, Ahmad Jamal, Frank Morgan, George Cables.

interviews.title: John Heard interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: when I was on the band, four and a half years, we had three bass players. They were all named John: it was John Heard, John Duke and John Clayton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: John Heard on bass. And now I have three bands you know... I get all the good players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Espinosa
about_quotes.evidence: John Heard, the great bass player, he was in the union one time and then he quit because the union wasn’t doing anything for him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Espinosa interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 20, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: Some other thing with John Heard on bass or just some of the better people here that are around that are available.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Houston Person Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember John Heard trying to define jazz for us...
about_quotes.doc_title: Houston Person Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Majesty of the Seas, May 29, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWEE] Wait a minute, you’re not talking about John Heard?
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Schenectady, New York, March 28, 1995
